subject: Cloud Computing
title: When to Choose SaaS
description: A 60-second lesson on When to Choose SaaS.

hook.text: Your company needs email, project management, and CRM. Building any of these yourself would be insane.

buildup.text: SaaS is the right choice when the problem is well-solved and building it isn't your competitive edge.

discovery.text: Using Gmail instead of running your own email server saves thousands of hours of maintenance.

twist.text: The risk is dependency. When Slack goes down, some companies literally cannot communicate.

climax.text: SaaS makes sense for everything that isn't your core product. Build what's unique, buy the rest.

punchline.text: Don't build what you can buy. Save your energy for what matters.

quiz.question: When is SaaS the right choice for a company?
options: When the capability isn't a competitive advantage and is well-solved by existing products
When the company wants full control over the underlying infrastructure
When data privacy requirements prevent using any external services
